formations_blocks: Jordan in a deep defensive setup/low block when defending
Algeria pressing higher and attacking in waves early on
Algeria circulating possession at the back to control tempo
adjustments: Algeria quickly moving from early direct attacks to calmer buildup through the back
Jordan transitioning from defending deep to releasing quick counterattacks
key_matchups: Nizar Al-Rashdan vs Amine Gouiri in midfield/pressing duels
Riyad Mahrez vs Jordan last-line defender(s) as Algeria look for him high up
Ehsan Haddad vs Algeria’s right-sided attacks and wide breaks
Musa Al-Taamari vs Algeria’s outnumbering defenders on the counter
phases.summary: The opening 15 minutes were played at a high tempo, with both sides producing early chances and showing contrasting approaches: Jordan looked to defend compactly and counter, while Algeria attacked quickly and also tried to settle possession through the back. Algeria’s press began to bite, especially through Gouiri’s work off the ball, while Jordan’s clearest break came from a turnover deep in their own half, though the resulting three-on-one made the counter difficult to sustain.

adjustments: Jordan blocking central entry routes and allowing Algeria time on the ball
Jordan threatening more on quick attacks down the right
Algeria maintaining patience but remaining stuck on the edge of the final third
key_matchups: Riyad Mahrez vs Jordan back line / keeper Yazeed Abulaila
Ali Olwan vs Algeria defensive side on Jordan's right
phases.summary: Jordan spent much of the phase in a compact 5-4-1, denying Algeria easy access into the final third and forcing them to circulate possession outside the block. Algeria had more of the ball and did create the clearest chance through Mahrez, but Jordan looked increasingly dangerous on their right side and eventually turned that defensive resilience into the opening goal through Al-Rashdan.

Algeria had the larger share of the ball at half-time, but the text suggests they were largely circulating possession without enough threat, as shown by a sloppy long cross before the break. Jordan, with much less of the ball, were more incisive and made their opportunities count, leading 1-0 thanks to Nizar Al-Rashdan's 36th-minute opener despite being out-possessed.

formations_blocks: Jordan defending with five at the back
Jordan in a compact defensive shape to protect the lead
Algeria chasing the game and pushing men between the lines
adjustments: Algeria made two changes at 48' with Nabil Bentaleb and Nadhir Benbouali replacing Hicham Boudaoui and Ramiz Zerrouki
Jordan settled into a five-at-the-back defensive approach
Algeria increased their use of central and half-space attacks to try to pull Jordan out of shape
key_matchups: Riyad Mahrez delivering dangerous crosses from set-piece situations against Jordan goalkeeper Abulaila
Ibrahim Maza driving through midfield and around the right side of the Jordan defence
Musa Al-Taamari breaking forward against Algeria's retreating defensive line
Ali Olwan and Musa Al-Taamari combining as Jordan's main counter-attacking threats
phases.summary: Algeria responded to going behind by making early changes and trying to attack more directly between the lines, but Jordan stayed organized in a five-at-the-back structure and were comfortable absorbing pressure. The match became a counter-attacking contest, with both sides threatening in transition before Algeria finally found the equalizer through Benbouali in the 69th minute.

formations_blocks: Algeria in a game-management mode after taking the lead, slowing the game and later banking back/retreating into a defensive block
Jordan pushing on in a more attacking, desperate shape to chase the equaliser
Algeria able to sit off and allow Jordan limited access through midfield, then defend around the ball near the corner flag
adjustments: Algeria substituted Riyad Mahrez for Anis Hadj Moussa to add fresh legs and pace
Jordan removed Mahmoud Al-Mardi and introduced Odeh Al-Fakhouri to increase attacking threat
Algeria made a double defensive change after scoring: Amine Gouiri and Rayan Aït-Nouri off, Jaouen Hadjam and Zineddine Belaid on to protect the lead
Algeria slowed the tempo and managed the clock after going ahead
key_matchups: Anis Hadj Moussa vs Jordan goalkeeper Abulaila, who saved his shot but could not stop the corner
Amine Gouiri vs Jordan’s defensive marking at the set-piece that led to the goal
Jordan’s right-side break vs Algeria’s numbers around the ball near the corner flag
phases.summary: This phase swung on Algeria’s substitutions and set-piece pressure: fresh attacking legs from Hadj Moussa helped generate danger, and Gouiri’s instincts from a crowded corner situation produced the decisive 82nd-minute goal. After taking the lead, Algeria immediately shifted into containment mode with defensive changes and slower tempo, while Jordan’s late push lacked a clear route through Algeria’s compact, game-managing block.
